title: Krieg Unleashes Poison Gas
scene: On pages 9–12, the Baratie conflict focuses on Luffy, Sanji, Don Krieg, Gin, Zeff. The Baratie battle turns into a test of will as Luffy keeps challenging Krieg despite poison, weapons, and exhaustion.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Patty! Don't you have some antidote!?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: U-Uhh yeah... I think so but that's mainly for food poisoning...

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: And besides, that guy's the enem...

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: Whatever! Just bring it over now!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: You idiot, just put that mask on him right now.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: It must contain at least a bit of an antidote of some sort.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: take him up to the 2" floor and have him breathe with that mask.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: If there's even the slightest chance of still saving him, then that's the first step to take.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Patty! Carne! Move your asses!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: A-Alright, you don't have to yell!

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: me too?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Don't you dare die, gin...!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Hmph, it's futile...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: He'll only hang around for another hour or so...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Don't you dare let yourself,

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Be killed by a guy like him!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Hang in there with sheer willpower! Got it!?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: because I'll send him flying in the meanwhile.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: N... No, stop... you can't...

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: Hope to... beat him...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Hey! Calm down, you idiot! Charging directly at him is exactly what he wants!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: You'll get yourself killed!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I'm not gonna die.

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: If you wanna shoot at me, then go ahead! Shoot as much as you want!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Hey!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Fool...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: nothing easier to kill than a worked up idiot...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Fine! Do whatever the hell you want...!

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: I've heard that even monkeys learn from their mistakes,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: But I guess you're even dumber than a monkey seeing as how you're trying to repeat your mistake a second time.

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: ain't that right, hammer boy!?

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: I'm just bad at giving up, that's all.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: The ocean is your doom! But if you try to jump, my Spears will make mineseat out of you!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Beyond the fins of that restaurant lies your grave!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: My grave!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: He's Shoo- Ting His bombs again!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: He's gonna make a screen!

summary: This part keeps the Baratie battle centered on willpower, debt, hunger, and pride. The dialogue shows who still stands firm after Krieg’s violence and who is ready to break away from the restaurant’s old life.
